reviews.review_msg: good prof. lectures enjoyable but disorganized. no point really taking notes just sit back and try to absorb the material. midterm/final were long but marked fairly.

Amazing professor! makes a 3 hour lecture fun through real examples and jokes. Realizes students have other courses than 360 to worry about so doesn't give ridiculous assignments. Gives great and supportive feedback and overall was a great experience. If you're afraid of 360 take it with Robin cause he is hands down the best professor for the class
horrible professor, never help his student even there's unfair in-group members number, take him if you wanna ruin your GPA!

Totally ineffective lectures. Assignments and rubric are vague. Proper assignments should have all you need in the rubric or assignment description. Kinda funny but goes on ill informed political rants. Streams videos from his cave.

I found Robin was extremely disorganized with his lecture material, canvas structure, and syllabus as well as due dates! He seems caring and is very funny which helps make the class more fun but grading is extremely tough and very little things will dock you big points. However, this may just be the TAs.

Funny and relatable. However, he does not follow his powerpoints or textbook at all. It can be very disorganized and confusing. He did give us the option of an take home exam or in class, and we chose take home. The grading criteria is very vague and ambiguous. Tough to actually satisfy.

Took both BUS 393 and BUS 360W with Robin, and I have got to say, he is an amazing prof. 393 can be a dry course but Robin made 1/4 of that class weighted towards interactive tutorial sessions (based on mini-cases). That being said, the final was long so study hard for it. Robin was very helpful for BUS 360. He wants every student to succeed.
Best professor at Simon Fraser University.

Robin gives interesting anecdotal lectures, but does not go over important key terms. He also changes his test criteria based on student feedback. Constantly adjusting our expectations (open book? closed book?) was very stressful. I'd urge him to just decide one thing and stick to it.

Awesome professor. I would love to take a class with him again. Explains the concepts very properly. The examples he uses in the class are great. He is very clear about what he expects in the midterm and final exams.
